name: SFMTA Board of Directors
role: Approves fare policy and provides policy oversight for Muni
timing_sensitivity: High during budget adoption and fare hearings
notes: Direct formal actor on fare policy. Official board page says it provides policy oversight for Muni. Source: https://www.sfmta.com/units/board-directors

name: SFMTA Director of Transportation
role: Leads agency strategy and public budget/fare proposals
timing_sensitivity: High during budget development and public outreach
notes: Agency leadership shapes what options are presented first. Julie Kirschbaum has publicly presented budget and revenue options. Source: https://www.sfcta.org/blogs/updates-regional-transportation-revenue-measure-and-muni-parcel-tax

name: Mayor of San Francisco
role: City executive; forwards SFMTA budget and can veto appropriations
timing_sensitivity: Very high around budget deadlines and ballot decisions
notes: Charter gives Mayor major leverage over appropriations and budget presentation. Source: https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/san_francisco/latest/sf_charter/0-0-0-920

name: San Francisco Board of Supervisors
role: City legislative body with budget rejection/override power and major agenda-setting capacity
timing_sensitivity: Very high during budget season and ballot-measure placement
notes: Critical funding gatekeeper; some members will be more pro-transit than others. Source: https://www.sf.gov/budget-process-documents-for-fiscal-years-2026-2027

name: Board of Supervisors Budget and Appropriations Committee
role: Filters city budget details before full Board action
notes: Operational choke point for budget negotiations even if not the final adopting body.

name: San Francisco Controller's Office
role: Convened/facilitated the Muni Funding Working Group and shapes fiscal framing
timing_sensitivity: High when deficit framing and options are published
notes: Important credibility actor for what counts as fiscally serious. Source: https://www.sfmta.com/projects/muni-funding-working-group

name: San Francisco County Transportation Authority
role: Transit funding policy body and venue for local/regional revenue strategy
timing_sensitivity: High leading into 2026 regional/local revenue measures
notes: Key institutional vehicle for advancing revenue proposals. Source: https://www.sfcta.org/blogs/updates-regional-transportation-revenue-measure-and-muni-parcel-tax

name: Metropolitan Transportation Commission
role: Regional funding and coordination body administering bridge-loan and regional transit strategy
timing_sensitivity: High during regional-measure design and loan administration
notes: Important because MTC administers regional funding structures and the emergency loan. Source: https://mtc.ca.gov/news/governor-signs-bill-authorizing-loan-bay-area-transit-agencies

name: Governor/CalSTA
role: State executive actors able to structure bridge finance and shape transit legislation
timing_sensitivity: Medium; high when emergency state actions are needed
notes: The 2026 loan showed state willingness to prevent service collapse, but not to permanently backfill local operations. Source: https://www.gov.ca.gov/2026/02/19/governor-newsom-signs-legislation-authorizing-590-million-emergency-loan-to-bay-area-transit/

name: Transit riders and rider coalitions
role: Direct users; public-comment base; can legitimize or delegitimize policy sequencing
timing_sensitivity: High when cuts or fare increases are proposed
notes: Riders often support affordability but can oppose fare-free proposals if they fear service degradation first.

name: Youth advocates and school/community organizations
role: Natural base for expanding an already existing youth-access frame
timing_sensitivity: Medium to high when school access and youth mobility are salient
notes: Youth Free Muni gives these groups a strong local precedent.

name: Senior and disability advocates
role: Can argue from mobility justice, ADA access, and existing Free Muni precedents
notes: Particularly influential if proposal is targeted before universal.

name: Transit labor unions
role: Represent operators and workers affected by service, enforcement, and staffing choices
timing_sensitivity: High when service cuts or layoffs are on the table
notes: Could support access expansion if paired with service protection; could resist if fare-free is seen as competing with staffing or reliability.

name: Downtown/business groups
role: Influence citywide economic narrative and ballot coalitions
timing_sensitivity: High if tax measures affect commercial property or business climate narratives
notes: May support transit stabilization but resist parcel taxes or broad new levies.

name: SPUR and policy think-tank voices
role: Shape elite-policy conversation on transit funding and reform
notes: Often important for making proposals sound serious to staff and media audiences.

name: Local media and editorial boards
role: Amplify viability frame, fiscal skepticism, or access narrative
timing_sensitivity: High during ballot and budget fights
notes: Narrative influence is large even though formal power is low.
